faq.question: What time is it in Houston right now?
faq.answer: Houston currently observes Central Standard Time (CST/UTC-6) from November through early March, and Central Daylight Time (CDT/UTC-5) from March through November. As the fourth-largest city in the United States and a major energy hub, Houston's time zone aligns with the central business corridor spanning from Texas to Illinois, facilitating coordination across America's industrial heartland.

faq.question: What time zone is Houston in?
faq.answer: Houston operates in the Central Time Zone (CT), specifically Central Standard Time (CST/UTC-6) during winter months and Central Daylight Time (CDT/UTC-5) during summer months. This timezone placement positions Houston one hour behind the Eastern Seaboard and one hour ahead of the Mountain states, making it strategically positioned for business across North American markets.

faq.question: Does Houston observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Houston observes Daylight Saving Time annually. Clocks spring forward one hour at 2:00 AM on the second Sunday in March (shifting to UTC-5) and fall back one hour at 2:00 AM on the first Sunday in November (returning to UTC-6). This practice affects Houston's 2.3 million residents and impacts coordination with the city's 23 Fortune 500 company headquarters.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Houston and New York?
faq.answer: Houston is 1 hour behind New York year-round. When it's noon in New York (Eastern Time), it's 11:00 AM in Houston (Central Time). Both cities observe Daylight Saving Time on the same schedule, maintaining this consistent one-hour offset. This timezone relationship is critical for Houston's financial sector coordinating with Wall Street and for the 53 daily direct flights between the cities.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Houston and London?
faq.answer: Houston is 6 hours behind London during winter months (CST vs GMT) and 5 hours behind during summer when both observe daylight saving time. When it's 5:00 PM in London, it's 11:00 AM in Houston in winter or noon in summer. This offset is essential for Houston's energy sector coordinating with European markets, as the city handles over 40% of U.S. petroleum refining capacity.

faq.question: What time does the stock exchange open in Houston?
faq.answer: The New York Stock Exchange opens at 8:30 AM Central Time in Houston (9:30 AM Eastern Time). Houston-based trading desks and the offices of 23 Fortune 500 companies begin pre-market analysis as early as 7:00 AM CT to prepare for the opening bell. The Houston energy trading sector, which influences global oil prices, operates from 7:30 AM CT to align with both U.S. financial markets and international energy exchanges.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Houston from the US?
faq.answer: The optimal time to call Houston from elsewhere in the US depends on your timezone: from the East Coast, call between 10:00 AM-6:00 PM EST (9:00 AM-5:00 PM Houston time); from the West Coast, call between 9:00 AM-4:00 PM PST (11:00 AM-6:00 PM Houston time). Houston's business community typically maintains standard 9:00 AM-5:00 PM CT hours, though the energy sector often operates extended hours from 7:00 AM-6:00 PM CT to overlap with international markets.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Houston?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Houston run 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Central Time, Monday through Friday. However, Houston's energy sector frequently operates 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM CT to coordinate with global oil markets. The Texas Medical Center, the world's largest medical complex, operates 24/7 with peak outpatient hours from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM CT. Retail establishments typically open 10:00 AM to 9:00 PM CT, while restaurants in Houston's diverse culinary scene often serve until 10:00 PM or later on weekends.

seo_description: Current time in Houston, Texas. Central Time Zone (CST/CDT UTC-6/-5). Live world clock, timezone converter, business hours, and DST schedule.
seo_title: Current Time in Houston — Central Time Zone, UTC Offset & Clock

content_blocks.title: Houston Time Zone Guide: Central Time Explained
content_blocks.content: Houston operates within the Central Time Zone (CT), observing Central Standard Time (CST/UTC-6) from the first Sunday in November through the second Sunday in March, then switching to Central Daylight Time (CDT/UTC-5) for the remainder of the year. As the fourth-largest city in the United States with a metropolitan population exceeding 7 million, Houston's timezone positioning is strategically significant for commerce spanning the Americas. The city sits at approximately 95.3°W longitude, placing it naturally within the zone designated for UTC-6 during standard time. This central positioning makes Houston a critical coordination point for businesses operating across multiple U.S. time zones—the city serves as headquarters to 23 Fortune 500 companies including energy giants like ConocoPhillips and Halliburton. Houston's timezone facilitates morning overlap with Eastern financial markets while maintaining afternoon availability for West Coast technology partners. The city's role as America's energy capital means its business hours (typically 7:30 AM to 6:00 PM CT in the energy sector) directly influence global oil prices, with morning trading activity in Houston affecting markets from London to Singapore. For international coordination, Houston maintains predictable offsets: 1 hour behind New York, 2 hours ahead of Los Angeles, 6 hours behind London in winter (5 in summer), and 14 hours behind Tokyo. Compare Houston's time with other major cities using our timezone converter at /time/houston/new-york/ and plan international meetings effectively.

content_blocks.title: Business Hours in Houston: Energy Capital Operations
content_blocks.content: Houston's business landscape operates on distinctive schedules shaped by its status as the global energy capital and home to the world's largest medical center. Standard corporate hours run 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Central Time weekdays, but Houston's energy trading floors begin as early as 7:00 AM CT to catch the opening of European markets and often extend until 6:00 PM CT to overlap with Asian trading sessions. The Houston energy sector's extended hours reflect the city's processing of over 40% of U.S. petroleum refining capacity and its influence on global oil benchmarks. Financial institutions serving Houston's 23 Fortune 500 companies typically maintain 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM CT schedules, synchronized with New York Stock Exchange hours (8:30 AM CT opening). The Texas Medical Center, comprising 61 institutions including world-renowned facilities like MD Anderson Cancer Center, operates 24/7 with peak outpatient appointments scheduled 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM CT. Houston's retail sector generally opens 10:00 AM to 9:00 PM CT Monday through Saturday, extending to 10:00 PM Friday and Saturday in major shopping districts like The Galleria. The city's internationally acclaimed restaurant scene—Houston has more than 10,000 restaurants representing cuisines from over 70 countries—typically serves lunch 11:00 AM to 2:00 PM CT and dinner 5:30 PM to 10:00 PM CT, with many establishments staying open until 11:00 PM or midnight on weekends. Government offices maintain 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM CT schedules. When scheduling calls or meetings with Houston businesses, consider using /time/houston/london/ or /time/houston/tokyo/ to identify optimal overlap windows with international partners.

content_blocks.title: Calling Houston Internationally: Optimal Contact Windows
content_blocks.content: Successfully connecting with Houston from international locations requires understanding both timezone mathematics and the city's industry-specific schedules. From London (6 hours ahead in winter, 5 hours in summer), the ideal calling window is 3:00 PM to 11:00 PM GMT to reach Houston's 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CT business hours, though energy sector contacts are accessible from 1:00 PM GMT (7:00 AM CT). European callers should note that Houston's energy trading desks are most active 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM GMT (7:00 AM to 10:00 AM CT) when U.S. market opening volatility is highest. From Tokyo (15 hours ahead during Houston's standard time, 14 hours during daylight time), contact Houston between 11:00 PM JST and 7:00 AM JST the following day to reach standard business hours, or target 9:00 PM to midnight JST for energy sector early risers. Singapore (14 hours ahead in winter, 13 in summer) should call between 10:00 PM SGT and 6:00 AM SGT. Sydney faces the challenging offset of being 16-17 hours ahead, making early morning Sydney time (5:00 AM to 1:00 PM AEDT) correspond to Houston's afternoon and evening. For domestic U.S. callers, East Coast contacts should reach Houston 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM EST (9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CT), while West Coast callers have the convenience of 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM PST (11:00 AM to 6:00 PM CT). Houston's George Bush Intercontinental Airport operates as a United Airlines hub with direct flights to 180 destinations, making the city highly connected globally—consider scheduling calls around typical flight times for international business travelers. The Port of Houston, America's largest port by tonnage, operates 24/7, meaning maritime logistics contacts are reachable outside standard business hours. Calculate precise calling windows using our timezone comparison tool at /time/houston/singapore/ or /time/houston/sydney/ for accurate scheduling across Houston's diverse business sectors.

content_blocks.title: Houston Daylight Saving Time: Annual Clock Changes
content_blocks.content: Houston observes Daylight Saving Time in accordance with federal U.S. regulations, advancing clocks one hour at 2:00 AM Central Standard Time on the second Sunday of March (transitioning to Central Daylight Time/UTC-5) and retreating one hour at 2:00 AM Central Daylight Time on the first Sunday of November (returning to Central Standard Time/UTC-6). These biannual transitions affect Houston's 2.3 million city residents and 7.1 million metropolitan area inhabitants, impacting everything from NASA Johnson Space Center mission schedules to Port of Houston container operations. The spring transition effectively shifts Houston's sunrise from approximately 6:50 AM to 7:50 AM but extends evening daylight until 8:00 PM or later through summer—a benefit appreciated by Houston's outdoor recreation industry despite the city's subtropical climate. For businesses, the March time change is particularly significant: energy trading floors experience a compressed first hour as European markets have already been open for additional time, while the November change back extends the overlap with Asian markets. International companies coordinating with Houston must recalibrate twice annually—during Houston's Daylight Saving Time (mid-March to early November), the city is UTC-5, operating 5 hours behind London (which also observes summer time) and 13 hours behind Singapore. Outside these months, Houston is UTC-6, creating a 6-hour London offset and 14-hour Singapore offset. Not all global partners change simultaneously: while Houston, New York, and London all observe seasonal clock changes, much of Asia (including Singapore, Hong Kong, Tokyo) and Arizona do not, requiring careful calendar management. Houston's medical sector—particularly the 24/7 operations at Texas Medical Center facilities—implements detailed protocols for the time changes to ensure continuous patient care and medication scheduling. Technology systems across Houston's extensive energy infrastructure undergo coordinated updates to maintain real-time synchronization for pipeline operations, refinery processes, and trading systems. Use our meeting planner at /meeting/ to automatically account for DST transitions when scheduling recurring international calls with Houston-based partners throughout the year.

content_blocks.title: Houston's Global Business Connections by Timezone
content_blocks.content: Houston's economic profile as America's energy capital and fourth-largest city creates unique timezone coordination requirements with global markets. The city's 23 Fortune 500 company headquarters maintain critical connections spanning multiple continents, with operational hours designed to maximize overlap. Houston's morning hours (7:00 AM to 11:00 AM CT) provide crucial overlap with European business closings—when it's 7:00 AM in Houston, London markets are preparing to close at 1:00 PM GMT (winter) or noon BST (summer), allowing real-time energy commodity trading that influences global prices. The city's energy sector schedules morning conference calls during this window to catch European partners before end-of-day. Houston's afternoon (1:00 PM to 5:00 PM CT) aligns with West Coast business hours, facilitating coordination with California technology companies and Pacific Northwest aerospace partners. Evening hours in Houston (6:00 PM to 9:00 PM CT) overlap with early morning in Asia—when Houston's energy traders are reviewing after-hours developments at 8:00 PM CT, Singapore is starting its business day at 10:00 AM SGT. This asymmetric schedule means Houston's energy sector maintains staffed trading desks from 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM CT to cover a 20-hour global trading window. The Port of Houston, handling 285 million tons of cargo annually, coordinates with shipping partners across all time zones through 24/7 operations. NASA Johnson Space Center operates on Central Time but coordinates continuously with international space agencies, requiring mission control teams to work rotating shifts covering global time zones for International Space Station operations. Houston's medical sector attracts international patients from 150 countries, with the Texas Medical Center's international patient services operating extended hours to accommodate consultation calls from Middle Eastern (typically 7-9 hours ahead) and Asian (13-15 hours ahead) time zones. For companies establishing Houston operations, understanding these timezone dynamics is essential—compare Houston's time with your headquarters using our dedicated comparison pages like /time/houston/dubai/ for Middle East coordination or /time/houston/paris/ for European partnerships.

content_blocks.title: Houston Time Zone for Travelers: Jet Lag and Scheduling
content_blocks.content: Houston's two major airports—George Bush Intercontinental (IAH) and William P. Hobby (HOU)—serve over 70 million passengers annually, making timezone adjustment a common consideration for business and leisure travelers. Visitors arriving from the East Coast experience a relatively minor one-hour shift to Central Time, with body clocks naturally adjusting within a few hours—schedule important meetings after 10:00 AM CT on arrival day to ensure alertness. West Coast travelers arriving in Houston effectively gain two hours (when it's 9:00 AM in Los Angeles, it's 11:00 AM in Houston), creating an opportunity for productive afternoon schedules but potentially causing early morning wake-ups as bodies remain on Pacific time for the first day. Transatlantic travelers from Europe face more significant adjustment: arriving from London (6-hour time difference) typically lands passengers in Houston during afternoon hours, but their bodies perceive evening—plan to stay awake until at least 9:00 PM CT local time on arrival to begin proper circadian adjustment. European business travelers often schedule Houston meetings for late morning or early afternoon (10:00 AM to 2:00 PM CT), when their bodies still feel relatively alert despite timezone displacement. Asian travelers experience the most dramatic shift: arriving from Tokyo (15-hour difference) or Singapore (14-hour difference) effectively inverts day and night. Westbound travelers from Asia commonly arrive in Houston during evening or night hours—resist the temptation to sleep immediately upon afternoon arrivals, instead staying active until 10:00 PM CT to accelerate adjustment. Plan critical business meetings for at least 36 hours after arrival from Asia to ensure peak cognitive performance. Houston's subtropical climate with average temperatures of 70-80°F year-round can also affect circadian adjustment—the strong Texas sunlight helps reset biological clocks naturally, so maximize outdoor exposure during daylight hours after arrival. Our jet lag advisor at /jet-lag-advisor/ provides personalized adaptation schedules based on your origin city and arrival time. For departures, Houston's Central Time Zone positioning means eastbound overnight flights to Europe depart between 5:00 PM and 9:00 PM CT, arriving in the morning European time—these red-eye flights allow business travelers to arrive alert for same-day meetings if sleep is achieved during the flight. Calculate your arrival time at your destination relative to Houston using tools like /time/houston/los-angeles/ for domestic trips or /time/houston/london/ for international journeys.
